title: rasters
text:
rasters





Name
Description




Raster
A wrapper around the rasterio raster class, to provide easier access.





rasters.Raster(self, filepath, in_memory=False)
A wrapper around the rasterio raster class, to provide easier access.





Name
Description




band_count
Returns the number of bands in the raster.


bounds
Returns the bounds of the raster.


extract_multiband_patch
Extract a multiband patch (e.g. from a mosaic)


extract_multiband_patch_geo
Extract a multiband patch (e.g. from a mosaic) with an GeoPoint input (a point with a CRS)


extract_multiband_patch_ll
Extract a multiband patch (e.g. from a mosaic) with an LL input (a lat,lon in EPSG:4326)


extract_patch
Read a patch of the raster.


extract_patch_geo
Extracts a patch centered around a GeoPoint.


extract_patch_ll
Extracts a patch centered around the Lat,Lon in EPSG:4326.


extract_patch_pixels
Extracts a patch in pixels centred around (u,v)


get_bounds_as_geoarea
Returns the bounds of the raster as a GeoArea


get_crs
Return the crs of the raster.


get_geotransform
Returns the geotransform of the raster.


get_nodata
Return the nodata value of the raster.


get_stats
Gets the stats for the reward raster.


get_value_at_geo
Gets the value of a raster when specified with a GeoPoint.


get_value_at_ll
Gets the vale of a raster when specified with lat,lon with crs EPSG:4326.


get_value_at_location
Return the value at a given location, where the location is in the same coordinate system as the raster.


get_value_at_pixel
Return the value at a given pixel.


shape
Returns the shape of the raster.


valid_geoarea
Returns the valid area of the raster as a GeoArea


where
Find the indices where the condition is true.





rasters.Raster.band_count()
Returns the number of bands in the raster.





Type
Description





The number of bands in the raster.







rasters.Raster.bounds()
Returns the bounds of the raster.





Type
Description




(float, float, float, float)
The bounds of the raster.







rasters.Raster.extract_multiband_patch(xy, width, height, bands)
Extract a multiband patch (e.g. from a mosaic)





Name
Type
Description
Default




xy
XY
(situ.core.geometry.XY)
required


width
int
(int) patch size in pixels
required


height
int
(int) patch size in pixels
required


bands
Iterable[int]
[int] a list of bands to extract from. i.e. for a mosaic do list(range(1,3)).
required









Type
Description





np.array: the patch







rasters.Raster.extract_multiband_patch_geo(p, width, height, bands)
Extract a multiband patch (e.g. from a mosaic) with an GeoPoint input (a point with a CRS)





Name
Type
Description
Default




p

required


width
int
(int) width in pixels
required


height
int
(int) height in pixels
required


bands

required









Type
Description





np.array: the patch

Description

rasters.Raster.extract_patch(xy, width, height, band=1)
Read a patch of the raster.





Name
Type
Description
Default

XY

required

int

height
int

required


band
int

Description





np.ndarray: Patch of the raster.

Name

Default

required


band

Description

title: geometry
text:
geometry





Name
Description




GeoArea
A class to represent a geographical survey area.


GeoPath
A class to represent a path in geographical space.


GeoPoint
A class to represent a point in geographical space.


GeoPoints
A class to represent a list of points in geographical space.


GeoType
A base class to inherit from (makes typehinting easier)


LocalArea



LocalPath
A class to represent a path in local space.


LocalPoints
A collection of points in a local reference frame


XY
A local XY point





geometry.GeoArea(self, data, crs=None)
A class to represent a geographical survey area.
Examples:
# Create an area from an existing polygon
poly = shapely.geometry.Polygon([(151,-30), (151,-31), (150,-31), (150,-30)])
gpoly = gpd.GeoSeries(poly, crs="EPSG:4326")
area = GeoArea(gpoly)

# Create an area from a list of LLs
lls = [LL(-30,151), LL(-31,151), LL(-31,150), LL(-31,150)]
area = GeoArea(lls)

# Create an area from a list of points
points = [(151,-30), (151,-31), (150,-31), (150,-30)]
area = GeoArea(points, crs="EPSG:4326")





Name
Description




asarray
Returns the points as an array


centroid
Returns the centre of the survey area.


centroid_ll
Returns the centre of the survey area as LL


contains
Checks if the GeoArea contains another GeoArea, GeoPoint or GeoPoints.


explore
Views the path in an interactive map


from_file
Reads the GeoArea from a file, constructs a class


plot
Plots the GeoArea.


random_point_geo
Returns a random point within the survey area.


random_point_ll
Returns a random point within the survey area as LL.


to_crs
Returns a new Area object with a different CRS.


to_file
Writes the GeoArea to a file.


to_lls
Converts the GeoArea to a list of LLs defining the exterior coords


to_local
Converts to a local projection


to_points
Converts the GeoArea to a list of points.





geometry.GeoArea.asarray()
Returns the points as an array Returns: np.ndarray: The array



geometry.GeoArea.centroid()
Returns the centre of the survey area.





Type
Description




GeoPoint
The centroid.







geometry.GeoArea.centroid_ll()
Returns the centre of the survey area as LL





Type
Description




LL
The centroid.







geometry.GeoArea.contains(other)
Checks if the GeoArea contains another GeoArea, GeoPoint or GeoPoints. Args: other: (GeoPoint, GeoPoints, GeoArea) The other object.





Type
Description




bool
True if the GeoArea contains the other object.







geometry.GeoArea.explore(**kwargs)
Views the path in an interactive map





Type
Description





The folium map







geometry.GeoArea.from_file(filename, index=0)
Reads the GeoArea from a file, constructs a class





Name
Type
Description
Default




filename
str
(str) The filename to read from.
required


index
int
(int) the index of the geometry in the geodataframe.
0









Type
Description




GeoArea
The GeoArea







geometry.GeoArea.plot()
Plots the GeoArea. Returns: matplotlib.axes.Axes: The axes.



geometry.GeoArea.random_point_geo()
Returns a random point within the survey area. Returns: GeoPoint: The random point.



geometry.GeoArea.random_point_ll()
Returns a random point within the survey area as LL. Returns: LL: The random point.



geometry.GeoArea.to_crs(new_crs)
Returns a new Area object with a different CRS.



geometry.GeoArea.to_file(filename)
Writes the GeoArea to a file. Args: filename: (str) The filename to write to.





Type
Description




None
None







geometry.GeoArea.to_lls()
Converts the GeoArea to a list of LLs defining the exterior coords Returns: [LL]: The list of LLs.



geometry.GeoArea.to_local(origin)
Converts to a local projection Args: origin: (LL) The origin





Type
Description




LocalArea
The LocalArea in NED







geometry.GeoArea.to_points()
Converts the GeoArea to a list of points. Returns: [(x,y)]: The list of points.





geometry.GeoPath(self, points, crs=None)
A class to represent a path in geographical space.
Examples:
# Create a path from an existing GeoSeries
gpath = gpd.GeoSeries([shapely.geometry.LineString([(151, -30),(152, -31)]),
                       crs="EPSG:4326")
path = GeoPath(gpath)

# Create a path from a list of LLs
lls = [LL(-30,151), LL(-31,151)]
path = GeoPath(lls)

# Create a path from a list of points
points = [(151, -30), (152, -31)]
path = GeoPath(points, crs="EPSG:4326")





Name
Description




explore
Views the path in an interactive map


from_file
Reads the GeoPath from a file, constructs a class


length
Gets the distance of the GeoPath object.


plot
Plots the GeoPath.


rotate
Rotate the path around its centroid. Returns a new GeoPath object.


sample_points_along_path
Subsamples along the path with interval d


to_crs
To a new crs


to_csv
Writes the GeoPath to a csv file.


to_file
Writes the GeoPath to a file.


to_geopoints
Converts the GeoPath to a list of GeoPoints.


to_lls
Converts the GeoPath to a list of LLs.


to_local
Converts to a local projection


to_points
Converts the GeoPath to a list of points.


translate
Translate the path by a vector t. Returns a new LocalPath object.





geometry.GeoPath.explore(**kwargs)
Views the path in an interactive map





Type
Description





The folium map

geometry.GeoPoints(self, points, crs=None)
A class to represent a list of points in geographical space.
Examples:
    # Create a list of points from an existing GeoSeries
    gpoints = gpd.GeoSeries([shapely.geometry.Point(151, -30),
                            shapely.geometry.Point(152, -31)],
                            crs="EPSG:4326")
    points = GeoPoints(gpoints)

    # Create a list of points from a list of LLs
    lls = [LL(-30,151), LL(-31,151)]
    points = GeoPoints(lls)

    # Create a list of points from a list of points
    points = GeoPoints(points=[(151, -30), (152, -31)], crs="EPSG:4326")

title: geometry
text:
Name
Description




GeoArea
A class to represent a geographical survey area.


GeoPath
A class to represent a path in geographical space.


GeoPoint
A class to represent a point in geographical space.


GeoPoints
A class to represent a list of points in geographical space.


GeoType
A base class to inherit from (makes typehinting easier)


LocalArea



LocalPath
A class to represent a path in local space.


LocalPoints
A collection of points in a local reference frame


XY
A local XY point





geometry.GeoArea(self, data, crs=None)
A class to represent a geographical survey area.
Examples:
# Create an area from an existing polygon
poly = shapely.geometry.Polygon([(151,-30), (151,-31), (150,-31), (150,-30)])
gpoly = gpd.GeoSeries(poly, crs="EPSG:4326")
area = GeoArea(gpoly)

# Create an area from a list of LLs
lls = [LL(-30,151), LL(-31,151), LL(-31,150), LL(-31,150)]
area = GeoArea(lls)

# Create an area from a list of points
points = [(151,-30), (151,-31), (150,-31), (150,-30)]
area = GeoArea(points, crs="EPSG:4326")





Name
Description




asarray
Returns the points as an array


centroid
Returns the centre of the survey area.


centroid_ll
Returns the centre of the survey area as LL


contains
Checks if the GeoArea contains another GeoArea, GeoPoint or GeoPoints.


explore
Views the path in an interactive map


from_file
Reads the GeoArea from a file, constructs a class


plot
Plots the GeoArea.


random_point_geo
Returns a random point within the survey area.


random_point_ll
Returns a random point within the survey area as LL.


to_crs
Returns a new Area object with a different CRS.


to_file
Writes the GeoArea to a file.


to_lls
Converts the GeoArea to a list of LLs defining the exterior coords


to_local
Converts to a local projection


to_points
Converts the GeoArea to a list of points.

geometry.GeoPath(self, points, crs=None)
A class to represent a path in geographical space.
Examples:
# Create a path from an existing GeoSeries
gpath = gpd.GeoSeries([shapely.geometry.LineString([(151, -30),(152, -31)]),
                       crs="EPSG:4326")
path = GeoPath(gpath)

# Create a path from a list of LLs
lls = [LL(-30,151), LL(-31,151)]
path = GeoPath(lls)

# Create a path from a list of points
points = [(151, -30), (152, -31)]
path = GeoPath(points, crs="EPSG:4326")

title: geotypes
text:
geotypes is a python package for making it easy to work with geospatial data, particularly useful for geospatial planning and analysis.
Visit the documentation for installation, getting started, tutorials and API reference.


To get started with geotypes, check out the tutorials, which run you through the data types and how to use them.

title: geotypes
text: To get started with geotypes, check out the tutorials, which run you through the data types and how to use them.

title: Data Types
text:
Geotypes adds some high-level wrappers around geopandas and shapely to allow for easier use. They allow easy conversion between georeference schemes, better tracking of what coordinate system the variables are in and easy conversion between types. These include:
import geotypes

title: Data Types
text:
GeoPoint
The GeoPoint is an x,y coordinate with an associated Coordinate Reference System (CRS). It allows easy conversion to LL and XY data types.

p = geotypes.geometry.GeoPoint(x=151.28826000, y=-33.79798000, crs="epsg:4326") # initialise a lat, lon centred in Manly.
print("GeoPoint", p)
ll = p.to_ll() # Converts to lat,lon data type
print("In LL", ll)
p_utm = p.to_crs("EPSG:32756")  # converts to utm zone 56S
print("In UTM Zone 56S", p_utm)

GeoPoint GeoPoint(x=151.28826,y=-33.79798,crs=epsg:4326)
In LL LL(lat=-33.79798, lon=151.28826)
In UTM Zone 56S GeoPoint(x=341542.2378026622,y=6258926.312196327,crs=EPSG:32756)

title: Data Types
text:
LL
The lat,lon data type is there for convience and to make it explicit about what is latitude, what is longitude.

ll = geotypes.geometry.LL(-33.79798000,151.28826000)
print(ll)
# Convert to geopoint
p = ll.to_geopoint()
print(p)

LL(lat=-33.79798, lon=151.28826)
GeoPoint(x=151.28826,y=-33.79798,crs=EPSG:4326)



GeoPath
A path in geographic space, effectively a geopandas geoseries / shapely linestring with an associated CRS. It is commonly used to represent sampling transects (such as AUV transects). It has some convient functions to collect samples at set distances along the path - which can be done no-matter what

# Define a set of points. This is a simple broad grid in CRS "EPSG:32756"
points = [(342041.99358729343, 6259392.849573873),
 (342495.27623430215, 6259603.771833958),
 (342579.64503957686, 6259422.458960008),
 (342126.36269032373, 6259211.536449324),
 (342210.73189359176, 6259030.223443877),
 (342664.01394509675, 6259241.146205157)]

# create the geopath object
geopath = geotypes.geometry.GeoPath(points, crs="EPSG:32756")
print(geopath)

# Iterating over it returns a geopoint.
print("Geopoints:")
for p in geopath:
    print(p)
print("\n")

# Easily convert to other crs
geopath4326 = geopath.to_crs("EPSG:4326")
print(geopath4326)

# Sample points along the geopath
geopath_subsampled = geopath.sample_points_along_path(d=20) # sample a point every 20 meters.
print("Number of points in original {}, in subsampled {}".format(len(geopath), len(geopath_subsampled)))

GeoPath (crs= EPSG:32756): 
    (342041.99358729343, 6259392.849573873)
    (342495.27623430215, 6259603.771833958)
    (342579.64503957686, 6259422.458960008)
    (342126.36269032373, 6259211.536449324)
    (342210.73189359176, 6259030.223443877)
    (342664.01394509675, 6259241.146205157)

Geopoints:
GeoPoint(x=342041.99358729343,y=6259392.849573873,crs=EPSG:32756)
GeoPoint(x=342495.27623430215,y=6259603.771833958,crs=EPSG:32756)
GeoPoint(x=342579.64503957686,y=6259422.458960008,crs=EPSG:32756)
GeoPoint(x=342126.36269032373,y=6259211.536449324,crs=EPSG:32756)
GeoPoint(x=342210.73189359176,y=6259030.223443877,crs=EPSG:32756)
GeoPoint(x=342664.01394509675,y=6259241.146205157,crs=EPSG:32756)


GeoPath (crs= EPSG:4326): 
    (151.2937399882331, -33.79384884712429)
    (151.29867207410172, -33.79201494327435)
    (151.2995507283774, -33.793662084087515)
    (151.2946185667885, -33.795496022572)
    (151.29549717899934, -33.797143191322725)
    (151.30042941631163, -33.7953092181998)

Number of points in original 6, in subsampled 95




GeoArea
A GeoArea is a wrapper around a geopandas GeoSeries with just one element, a polygon or multipolygon. They are used within the situ framework to represent sampling bounds or focal areas. As such, they have easy access to random sampling.

# Define a geoarea
geoarea = geotypes.geometry.GeoArea([(151.28412747953516, -33.78781691040293),
 (151.30572252046485, -33.78781691040293),
 (151.30572478467255, -33.805848131225765),
 (151.28412521532746, -33.805848131225765),
 (151.28412747953516, -33.78781691040293)], crs='EPSG:4326')

# get some samples from within it - these are GeoPoints:
for n in range(5):
    print(geoarea.random_point_geo())
# you can get also get points directly as LLs, regardless of the CRS.
for n in range(5):
    print(geoarea.random_point_ll())

GeoPoint(x=151.2949853589017,y=-33.797472750334485,crs=EPSG:4326)
GeoPoint(x=151.29360291673302,y=-33.7924976125184,crs=EPSG:4326)
GeoPoint(x=151.29673124174616,y=-33.797515363166845,crs=EPSG:4326)
GeoPoint(x=151.28598012497667,y=-33.800236022988315,crs=EPSG:4326)
GeoPoint(x=151.30053260198127,y=-33.80065995600944,crs=EPSG:4326)
LL(lat=-33.79393601328281, lon=151.28993688424396)
LL(lat=-33.805312050042566, lon=151.28864575482626)
LL(lat=-33.795344282699155, lon=151.2912092214564)
LL(lat=-33.805730540875636, lon=151.29804060329622)
LL(lat=-33.7935942712713, lon=151.2932338029548)



# Plot the overall area on the map
import folium
# Plot the area
m = geoarea.explore(style_kwds={'fill': False, 'color':'green'})
# Plot the geopath
geopath.explore(m=m)
# Plot the samples along the geopath
geotypes.geometry.GeoPoints(geopath.sample_points_along_path(50).to_geopoints()).explore(m=m)
# Plot some random samples from the geoarea
geotypes.geometry.GeoPoints([geoarea.random_point_ll() for _ in range(10)]).explore(m=m, style_kwds={'color':'red'})
folium.LayerControl().add_to(m)
display(m)


Make this Notebook Trusted to load map: File -> Trust Notebook

title: Data Types
text:
Local Equivalents
There are local equivalents to the classes presented above. These include: - XY: Equivalent of GeoPoint - LocalPoints: Equivalent of GeoPoints - LocalPath: Equivalent of GeoPath - LocalArea: Equivalent of GeoArea These are just wrappers around the shapely geometry classes, but offer some extra functionality, including being able to convert to and from the Geo equivalents.

origin_ll = geoarea.centroid_ll()
# Convert the position to local
xy = p.to_local(origin_ll)
# Now go back to GeoPoint, this time with a different CRS.
pll = xy.to_geo(origin_ll, "epsg:4326")
# Print all to compare
print(p, xy, pll)


# Convert a GeoPath to a LocalPath
localpath = geopath.to_local(origin_ll)
# And go back again
geopath2 = localpath.to_geo(origin_ll, geopath.crs)

GeoPoint(x=342664.01394509675,y=6259241.146205157,crs=EPSG:32756) XY(x=509.7408123251043, y=168.9840842232932) GeoPoint(x=151.30042941631163,y=-33.7953092181998,crs=epsg:4326)


/home/jhs/environments/general/lib/python3.10/site-packages/geotypes/geometry.py:1407: UserWarning:

Geometry is in a geographic CRS. Results from 'centroid' are likely incorrect. Use 'GeoSeries.to_crs()' to re-project geometries to a projected CRS before this operation.

title: features
text:
features





Name
Description

Gathers raw features from a raster.





features.FeaturesFromRaster(self, raster_registry)
Gathers raw features from a raster.





Name
Description




check_valid
Checks if a sample set is valid.


get_output_format
Returns the output format of the feature vector, where the length corresponds to the length of the feature vector.


get_z
Gets the features at a given lat/lon


get_zs
Gets the features at a given set of lat/lon points

Type
Description
Default

Union[GeoPoint, LL, GeoPoints, GeoPath]
(GeoPoint, LL, GeoPoints, GeoPath) The samples to check.
required









Type
Description





True if the samples are valid, False otherwise.







features.FeaturesFromRaster.get_output_format()
Returns the output format of the feature vector, where the length corresponds to the length of the feature vector. i.e. if the feature vector contains [depth,slope,aspect,rugosity] this function would return [“depth”,“slope”,“aspect”,“rugosity”] Returns: [str]: maps index to feature vector item.
